messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following AI-powered hiring tool privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"TalentScore uses artificial intelligence to analyse CVs, video interviews, and psychometric test results to score and rank job candidates. Scores are provided to employers to assist hiring decisions. We process your data on behalf of the employer who directed you to this platform. Data is retained for 12 months. You may request human review of your score."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [FAIL] No lawful basis stated by TalentScore. As a processor, the lawful basis is the employer's responsibility, but the notice does not clarify this or confirm the employer has provided one.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [FAIL] Psychometric and video interview analysis can reveal personality traits, mental health indicators, and potentially protected characteristics. No acknowledgement or safeguard is stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Candidate scoring and ranking for hiring decisions is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PARTIAL] CV, video interview, and psychometric data are described as inputs, but the scope of inferences drawn is not defined.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 12-month retention stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Right to human review of automated score (Art. 22) is stated. Rights to access, erasure, portability, restriction, and objection are absent.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Employers named as score recipients. TalentScore's own sub-processors not disclosed.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Systematic automated processing of candidate data at scale requires a DPO and likely a DPIA under Art. 35.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (psychometric and video data may reveal special category information), Art. 22 (automated decision-making — DPIA required, rights incomplete), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent), Art. 28 (AI sub-processors not disclosed), Art. 46 (transfer information absent), Art. 37 (DPO and DPIA required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Clarify controller/processor relationship with employers and confirm lawful basis chain
- Acknowledge and address special category data risk in psychometric and video analysis
- Conduct and publish a DPIA for AI-based candidate scoring
- Add full data subject rights section
- Disclose AI infrastructure and cloud hosting sub-processors
- Address international transfer safeguards
- Appoint and disclose DPO
- Add breach notification commitment
